perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof. Renewals will be at Fazync’s sole discretion, irrespective of any prior notices or agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement auto-renews unless either party provides 90 days' notice. The modified clause gives Fazync sole discretion on renewal, overriding the automatic renewal and negating the need for notice. This introduces an in-text contradiction, making it unclear under what conditions the contract renews.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof. Renewals will be at Fazync’s sole discretion, irrespective of any prior notices or agreements. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states renewals are at Fazync's sole discretion irrespective of prior notices. The contradicted text mirrors this statement. The original text guaranteed renewal unless notice was given. This change creates a direct conflict by removing the guaranteed renewal and granting sole discretion, thus contradicting the original contract's terms for renewal.

perturbation.original_text: Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by Fazync at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at Fazync bank account.
perturbation.changed_text: Bravatek may receive a lead-finder fee at Fazync's sole discretion. Payment terms, if any, will be determined on a project-by-project basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a lead-finder fee between 10-20% to Bravatek. The modification replaces this with 'may receive a fee at Fazync's sole discretion,' making the fee optional rather than an agreed-upon condition. The phrase 'Payment terms, if any' further destabilizes the original payment term, creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about Bravatek's compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with Fazync via email to IHSI's CEO, Devon Jones, and delivered through Bravatek or a Fazync -designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek may receive a lead-finder fee at Fazync's sole discretion. Payment terms, if any, will be determined on a project-by-project basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a lead-finder fee between 10-20%. The changed text provides that Bravatek 'may receive a lead-finder fee at Fazync's sole discretion.' This clearly contradicts the original agreement by making the fee optional and based on Fazync's discretion, while the original text implied a guaranteed fee (within a range). The contradicted text also states that 'Bravatek may receive a lead-finder fee at Fazync's sole discretion,' but this is the result of the change. The original text implied a guaranteed payment. The modification effectively nullifies the expectation of a guaranteed lead-finder fee, creating a direct conflict regarding compensation.

perturbation.original_text: Fazync and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Fazync and Bravatek will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain confidence, however, Confidential Information may be shared internally within each company's subsidiaries and affiliates without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates strict confidentiality. The modified clause allows for the sharing of Confidential Information within each company's subsidiaries and affiliates, which is contradictory. This introduces an in-text contradiction since the original clause prohibits disclosure, while the updated one permits it under certain circumstances. This directly weakens the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. Fazync and Bravatek will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain confidence, however, Confidential Information may be shared internally within each company's subsidiaries and affiliates without restriction. The obligation of non-disclosure shall not apply to the following: a. Information at or after such time that is publicly available through no fault of either party b. Information at or after such time that is disclosed to either party by a third party entitled to disclose such information c. Information which is required by law to be disclosed to federal, state or local authorities.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates strict confidentiality, whereas the changed text allows Confidential Information to be shared within the company's subsidiaries and affiliates without restriction. The contradicted text defines confidential information broadly. The updated clause directly weakens the confidentiality obligations and creates a clear contradiction as it permits disclosure of information that was initially meant to be strictly confidential, leading to an in-text contradiction.
